items.name: Oracle
items.note: giving back most of its June AI-guidance pop

items.name: Tesla (Optimus)
items.note: UBS raised price target to $442 on physical AI/Optimus

outlook.summary: Not financial advice — this is a read of the public data and news above, meant to help you decide, not a recommendation. Always size positions and confirm with your own research.

strongest.reason: Memory and AI-adjacent names (Micron, Sandisk, Marvell) rallied on unabated AI demand commentary, with AI executives like Pat Gelsinger calling demand 'almost unlimited.' The PHLX chip index is up ~60% YTD despite bouts of volatility, and Arm's +9% move shows risk appetite is still there for the right names.

Bitcoin is holding near $64k despite ongoing Iran-related geopolitical noise, with spot ETF flows turning positive again and BlackRock's tokenized fund (BUIDL) AUM climbing. Stability under stress is itself a signal here.

weakest.reason: Oracle gave back a large chunk of its June AI-guidance rally, and broader chip-stock sentiment has been whipsawed by Meta's reported move to sell excess AI compute capacity and a Samsung earnings miss that triggered a semis selloff. The 'unlimited AI capex' story is being tested in real time.

The agentic-AI-eats-SaaS overhang persists; even as Salesforce and ServiceNow ship real integration work (Slack-to-Salesforce platform tie-in), the group hasn't found a durable floor. ServiceNow's move today was only modestly positive despite the news.

outlook.nextStep: The chip-sector split — memory/AI-infra names catching a bid while Oracle and parts of mega-cap cloud wobble on capacity-glut headlines — is the most worth a closer look right now, since it's a genuine divergence in the AI-demand narrative rather than a uniform move. AI software and robotics remain better watched than acted on: the former is still searching for a floor, and the latter (Tesla's price-target hike aside) is still more story than delivered numbers.

signals.title: Fed holds steady, oil and yields stay elevated on Iran tensions
signals.body: The Fed held its target rate at 3.50%-3.75% for a fourth straight meeting in June; the next FOMC decision lands July 28-29. Fed Chair Kevin Warsh said inflation risks have eased somewhat but flagged that some officials still see a case for a hike. Meanwhile renewed U.S.-Iran strikes have kept WTI crude near $71 and the 10-year yield near 4.56%, both headwinds for richly-valued growth names.
signals.watch: The July 28-29 FOMC decision and any further escalation around the Strait of Hormuz.
signals.focus: This is the swing factor for everything else this month — a hawkish surprise or oil spike would pressure growth names across every tier.

signals.title: Chip sector split: memory rallying, capacity-glut fears hit others
signals.body: Micron, Sandisk, Marvell, Broadcom, Intel, and AMD all traded higher this week on unabated AI-demand commentary, but the broader group has also seen sharp selloffs tied to a Samsung earnings miss and reports that Meta plans to sell excess AI computing capacity — raising the first real capacity-glut questions of this cycle.
signals.watch: Whether the Meta capacity story is a one-off or the start of a broader hyperscaler capex reassessment.
signals.focus: Worth a closer look on the memory/AI-infra names showing real demand-driven strength; the capacity-glut headlines are worth tracking before adding broadly.

signals.title: Oracle gives back its June AI-guidance pop
signals.body: Oracle shares have pulled back sharply from the highs reached after its June AI-cloud guidance beat, now trading well below that peak as investors reassess how much of the AI-infra buildout translates to near-term Oracle cloud revenue.
signals.watch: Oracle's next cloud-infrastructure booking update and any read-through to other hyperscaler capex plans.
signals.focus: Lower conviction right now on chasing the prior rally — the reversal suggests the market wants confirmation, not just guidance.

signals.title: Palantir upgraded; Salesforce-Slack integration finally ships
signals.body: DA Davidson upgraded Palantir to Buy citing ~39% upside, as CEO Alex Karp continues pushing a 'sovereign AI' narrative against large frontier labs. Separately, five years after acquiring Slack, Salesforce shipped a Slackbot integration tying together CRM data, Tableau analytics, and Data 360 customer profiles — a concrete product step for the AI-eats-SaaS debate.
signals.watch: Whether the Slack integration shows up in Salesforce's next seat-count and revenue commentary.
signals.focus: Worth watching for early proof points, but the sector-wide re-rating pressure hasn't lifted yet.

signals.title: Tesla gets a bullish price-target hike on physical AI
signals.body: UBS raised its Tesla price target to $442 from $364, crediting the company's investments in physical AI — including Optimus, Full Self-Driving, and Dojo — as underappreciated by the market. This follows last week's spike in humanoid-robotics names on Optimus procurement and Unitree IPO headlines.
signals.watch: Any concrete Optimus production or external-sale numbers, versus continued internal-testing-only status.
signals.focus: Speculative — sizeable analyst optimism, but actual commercial deployment is still the gap to watch.

signals.title: Bitcoin steady near $64k as ETF flows turn positive
signals.body: BTC is holding near $64,188 and ETH near $1,803 despite ongoing Iran-related geopolitical risk. Spot BTC ETF flows have reversed to net inflows (~$197M), and BlackRock's tokenized BUIDL fund crossed $900M on Avalanche, pushing total AUM to roughly $2.87B.
signals.watch: Continued ETF flow direction and any escalation in Middle East tensions that could reverse the trend.
signals.focus: Constructive trend this week — resilience under geopolitical stress is itself notable.

signals.title: CrowdStrike still trading close to its tracked stop
signals.body: CRWD is at $187.33, essentially flat versus recent sessions and still hovering just above the tracked stop at $184. Elsewhere in this tier, Unity Software has moved up meaningfully (~+8% since last check) while Zscaler and NetApp have been comparatively quiet.
signals.watch: Whether $184 holds on CRWD, and whether Unity's move has legs or fades.
signals.focus: Worth checking daily until CRWD clears back above $190 or confirms the stop.
